text.title: Structured Interview Design Procedure
text.summary: End-to-end 5-step procedure to apply Structured Interview Design. Each step pairs typed input + bounded action + named output + decision-gate. Steps are sequential unless explicitly marked parallel.

step.name: inventory-competencies
step.action: Pick 5-7 competencies; reject if any pair scores the same behavior.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

step.name: design-round-map
step.action: Assign each competency to a specific round with primary owner.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

step.name: build-scorecards
step.action: Build one scorecard per round, anchored to the rubric.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

step.name: dry-run
step.action: Run a dry-run with two interviewers; revise where disagreement >1 level.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

step.name: publish-debrief-contract
step.action: Document the debrief: who writes first, how dissent is recorded, decision rule.
step.decision-gate: Output present and matches `content/02-output-contract.xml` schema for this stage; otherwise block.

section.title: Failure handling
section.p: If a decision-gate blocks, do NOT proceed to the next step. Surface the blockage to the owner with the specific input that did not satisfy the gate. Resume only after the input is corrected.
